verses.book: On the Decease of His Brother Satyrus.
verses.text: Besides his elder sister Marcellina, who received the veil at the hands of Pope Liberius, at Christmas [perh.

verses.book: On the Decease of His Brother Satyrus.
verses.text: 353 a.d.], St. Ambrose had also a brother named Satyrus, to which name, in the epitaph on him ascribed to the bishop, is added Uranius.

verses.book: On the Decease of His Brother Satyrus.
verses.text: This is probably, however, merely in reference to his translation from earth to heaven.

verses.book: On the Decease of His Brother Satyrus.
verses.text: Satyrus had in his earlier years, as well as St. Ambrose, practised as an advocate, and held office.

verses.book: On the Decease of His Brother Satyrus.
verses.text: But when his brother was appointed Bishop of Milan, Satyrus at once gave up his appointment, and devoted his life to managing St. Ambrose’s secular affairs, that nothing might distract him from his episcopal duties.

verses.book: On the Decease of His Brother Satyrus.
verses.text: After however, a few short years of devotion to this task, he succumbed to a severe illness October 17, a.d.

verses.book: On the Decease of His Brother Satyrus.
verses.text: The grief of St. Ambrose at the loss of his absolutely like-minded brother was intense, and to it we owe the exquisite discourse delivered at the funeral of Satyrus, and the second, on the resurrection, delivered a week later.

verses.book: On the Decease of His Brother Satyrus.
verses.text: St. Ambrose subsequently revised these two addresses, and they have come down to us as the “two books of St. Ambrose on the decease of Satyrus,” some mss. adding, “and the resurrection of the dead.”

verses.book: On the Decease of His Brother Satyrus.
verses.text: The epitaph on Satyrus, said to be by St. Ambrose, is as follows:

verses.book: On the Decease of His Brother Satyrus.
On the Decease of His Brother Satyrus.
On the Decease of His Brother Satyrus.
verses.text: Hæc meriti merces, ut sacri sanguinis humor
